name: Blackboard Pattern

x.structure: Independent knowledge sources read and write a shared "blackboard" data store; a control component decides which source to activate next based on the blackboard’s evolving state.
antiPatterns: Blackboard as an unstructured global blob
Knowledge sources with hidden ordering dependencies
x.scale: Subsystem / application; AI, speech/vision, sensor fusion, planning.

def: An architecture for ill-defined problems where independent specialist components incrementally build a solution on a shared data store coordinated by a control strategy.
problem: Some problems (perception, planning) have no fixed algorithm; partial contributions from diverse experts must combine opportunistically.
mechanism: A shared blackboard holds the evolving solution state. Knowledge sources watch it and, when their preconditions match, contribute updates. A control component picks which source runs next, repeating until a solution emerges.
benefits: Handles problems with no deterministic algorithm
limits: Control strategy is hard to design / tune
Shared mutable state → coupling and concurrency issues
Hard to test and reason about
failures: Unstructured blackboard becoming a global junk drawer
whenNot: Problems with a known deterministic algorithm
myths: "Blackboard is obsolete" — it underpins many sensor-fusion and AI planning systems
